text: AWS CloudTrail recorded 2 API call(s) by "devops-admin" from IP(s) 185.217.143.91 in region us-east-1 between 2026-05-16T01:13:27 and 2026-05-16T01:16:54 UTC. At 2026-05-16T01:13:27, the StopLogging API was called to disable logging for trail "org-production-trail" via cloudtrail. This is a critical defense evasion technique (T1562.008 (Impair Defenses: Disable Cloud Logs)) commonly used by attackers to prevent their subsequent actions from being recorded. At 2026-05-16T01:16:54, 2 EC2 instance(s) of type g5.xlarge were launched (IDs: i-0f2c7d91a4b3ce921, i-0de7b4f90c18ab443). The use of Terraform as the user agent suggests infrastructure-as-code deployment. This activity sequence is highly suspicious: disabling audit logging followed by infrastructure changes from an external IP is a classic attacker playbook for establishing persistence while evading detection.

AWS CloudTrail recorded 1 API call(s) by "EC2AdminRole (session: ec2-instance)" from IP(s) 34.207.188.14 in region us-east-1 between 2026-05-16T01:21:43 and 2026-05-16T01:21:43 UTC. At 2026-05-16T01:21:43, security group sg-0cb33aa7de91f17c2 was modified to allow inbound TCP port 3333-3333 from 0.0.0.0/0. Opening port 0.0.0.0/0 exposes the service to the entire internet, which is a significant security risk. All API calls originated from external IP(s) 34.207.188.14, which warrants verification that this activity was authorized.
